result:
I've completed a thorough review of the working-tree change. Let me summarize my analysis.

The change removes the `globalThis.__NEXT_TRANSLATE__ = { lang, namespaces, config }` assignment from the pages-dir `appWithI18n` HoC, replacing it with an explanatory comment, plus adds a regression test.

I verified this against every consumer of that global:

- **`useTranslation.tsx:19-20`** — only switches to the frozen `createTranslation` (app-dir) path when the global has a `config`. With the global no longer set in pages-dir, it correctly falls through to the reactive `useTranslationInPages` (context-based) path. This is exactly the #1256 fix.
- **`getT.tsx:11`** — falls back to `getConfig()` when the global is absent, so server helpers still work.
- **`createTranslation.tsx`** — only reached when the global is set (app-dir via `AppDirI18nProvider`), unaffected.
- **`I18nProvider.tsx:16`** — calls `useTranslation()` for `parentLang`, but `lang` is always driven by the explicit `lng` prop first (`lng || parentLang || ...`), so behavior is preserved.

The removed assignment was also a global mutated during render — a cross-request leak risk on the server — so removing it is strictly an improvement. No other code or existing test (`getT.test.js`, `useTranslation.test.js` set the global themselves) depends on `appWithI18n` setting it. I also confirmed the new regression test genuinely fails under the old buggy behavior (frozen `lang='en'` + malformed nested namespaces would break the first assertion) and passes under the fix, with no conditional-hooks-order hazard introduced.

The change is clean and correct.

```json
{
  "claims": []
}
```
